title: Taints and Tolerations
description:
## Taints and Tolerations

Taints allow a node to **repel** pods. A toleration on a pod allows it to be scheduled onto a tainted node.

The node `k8s-lab` has been tainted with `env=gpu:NoSchedule`.

**Your task:**

Create a Pod named `gpu-pod` using `nginx:alpine` that tolerates the taint `env=gpu:NoSchedule` so it can be scheduled on this node.

```bash
kubectl describe node k8s-lab | grep -A5 Taints
```

hints.title: Toleration structure
hints.body: Add a `tolerations` block under `spec`. Match `key`, `operator`, `value`, and `effect` to the node taint.
hints.command:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: gpu-pod
spec:
  tolerations:
  - key: "env"
    operator: "Equal"
    value: "gpu"
    effect: "NoSchedule"
  containers:
  - name: app
    image: nginx:alpine
EOF

setup_commands.command: kubectl taint nodes k8s-lab env=gpu:NoSchedule --overwrite=true 2>/dev/null || true

teardown_commands.command: kubectl taint nodes k8s-lab env=gpu:NoSchedule- 2>/dev/null || true
